app_info.subtitle: Private memory vault
app_info.description: Marcus helps users save, search, update, link, and recall personal markdown notes through ChatGPT while storing the vault in the user's private GitHub repository.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a new markdown note in the user's private vault and returns the created file details.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private GitHub vault and does not publish public internet content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new file and does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Updates an existing vault note by replacing, appending, or prepending markdown content.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private GitHub vault and does not publish public internet content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Can replace existing note content, with optional SHA checking to reduce accidental overwrite conflicts.

justifications.read_only_justification: Removes a note by archiving it or permanently deleting it from the user's vault.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private GitHub vault and does not publish public internet content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Can permanently delete a selected vault note when hard delete mode is used.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches markdown notes and metadata in the user's vault without changing files.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads from the user's private GitHub vault and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Retrieves recent topic names, folder names, and tag counts without modifying vault content.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads from the user's private GitHub vault and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Retrieves the requested markdown note content without modifying the vault.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads from the user's private GitHub vault and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Lists vault folders and files without modifying any content.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads from the user's private GitHub vault and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Adds a timestamped entry to today's daily note or creates that note if it does not exist.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private GitHub vault and does not publish public internet content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Appends or creates note content and does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Stores a durable memory entry and links it from the daily note in the user's vault.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private GitHub vault and does not publish public internet content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates memory and daily-note entries and does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Archives a matching memory entry and writes an archive record in the user's vault.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private GitHub vault and does not publish public internet content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Hides the selected memory from future recall by marking it archived.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches active memory entries and returns matching records without modifying vault files.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads from the user's private GitHub vault and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Returns a compact markdown snapshot of active memories without modifying vault files.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads from the user's private GitHub vault and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Updates note frontmatter to add a wikilink and may create a stub target note.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private GitHub vault and does not publish public internet content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds links or a stub note and does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Retrieves recently changed note paths and timestamps without modifying vault content.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads from the user's private GitHub vault and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

test_cases.description: Save a new topic note with frontmatter.
test_cases.user_prompt: Save a note in my second brain about the book Atomic Habits with a short summary and the tag productivity.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates a markdown note in the private vault and returns the note path and identifier.

test_cases.user_prompt: Search my second brain for anything I saved about Cloudflare Workers deployment.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns matching vault notes with paths and metadata so ChatGPT can ground the answer in saved context.

test_cases.description: Open a specific note after search.
test_cases.user_prompt: Open the note at 20-topics/cloudflare-workers.md from my vault.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the requested markdown note content from the user's private vault.

test_cases.user_prompt: Add to today's daily note that I met Alex and we discussed the Q3 launch plan.
test_cases.expected_output: Appends the user's update to today's daily note or creates the daily note if needed.

test_cases.description: Store and later recall a durable memory.
test_cases.user_prompt: Remember that I prefer short direct standup updates, then recall memories about standup updates.
test_cases.expected_output: Stores the preference as an active memory and returns matching active memories for the recall query.

test_cases.description: Update an existing note with conflict protection.
test_cases.user_prompt: Append a new section to 40-projects/website-redesign.md with the latest launch checklist.
test_cases.expected_output: Updates the selected note according to the requested mode and returns the updated file details or a conflict if the SHA check fails.

test_cases.user_prompt: Forget the memory with block id id-a1b2c3 because that preference is outdated.
test_cases.expected_output: Marks the matching memory archived and records it in the memory archive.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for general web search.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Search the web for today's weather in Bali.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because the request is unrelated to the user's private vault.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for unrelated calendar management.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Move my 3 PM meeting to tomorrow and email the attendees.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because it does not manage calendars or send emails.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for public publishing.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Publish my latest project update as a public blog post.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because it stores private vault notes and does not publish public content.
